subject: Sociology
title: Civil Religion
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Civil Religion.

hook.text: Americans pledge allegiance to one nation under God. That's not Christianity — it's something else.

buildup.text: Robert Bellah described civil religion — sacred beliefs, symbols, and rituals about the nation itself.

discovery.text: Civil religion treats national history as sacred story. Founders become prophets. Wars become sacrifices.

twist.text: It unifies a diverse nation but also sacralizes power. Questioning the country feels almost blasphemous.

climax.text: Civil religion shows that secular societies still need sacred stories to bind people with shared meaning.

punchline.text: Nations have religions of their own.

quiz.question: What is 'civil religion' according to Bellah?
options: Sacred beliefs and rituals about the nation
